capabilities.name: CapabilityHTTPProxy
capabilities.doc: CapabilityHTTPProxy indicates that the SDK supports setting an HTTP proxy, through which the SDK will make all requests.

capabilities.name: CapabilityTLSCustomCA
capabilities.doc: CapabilityTLSCustomCA means the SDK is capable of establishing a TLS session and configuring peer verification to use a custom CA certificate. The path to this CA cert is provided to the SDK. The SDK should then configure this path as the only CA cert in its trust store (rather than adding it to an existing trust store.)

capabilities.name: CapabilityTLSSkipVerifyPeer
capabilities.doc: CapabilityTLSSkipVerifyPeer means the SDK is capable of establishing a TLS session but can be configured to skip the peer verification step. This allows the SDK to establish a connection with the test harness using a self-signed certificate without a CA. Not all SDKs have this capability.

capabilities.name: CapabilityTLSVerifyPeer
capabilities.doc: CapabilityTLSVerifyPeer means the SDK is capable of establishing a TLS session and verifying its peer. This is generally a standard capability of all SDKs. However, the additional tests this enables may cause the suite to run slower than normal and may cause unexpected behavior. Therefore, it should be manually tested first.

capabilities.name: CapabilityWrapper
capabilities.doc: CapabilityWrapper indicates that the SDK supports setting wrapper name and version and including them in request headers.
